message:
fix(fcp-bootstrap): bind soft-token key derivation to per-token + per-identity context

Prior to this change every `SoftTokenDriver` derived identity material
with a thin HKDF call:

    HKDF-SHA256(salt = DEFAULT_MASTER_SEED,
                ikm  = (identity_index as u64).to_le_bytes())
           .expand("fcp-soft-token-identity", …)

The implicit CA used a similar identity-free call:

    HKDF-SHA256(salt = DEFAULT_MASTER_SEED,
                ikm  = b"fcp-soft-token-implicit-ca")
           .expand("fcp-soft-token-ca", …)

This had two collisions:

1. Two tokens in the same process (slot 0 and slot 1, different
   labels/serials) derived *identical* identity material at the same
   index. `SoftTokenHarness::new(vec![cfg_a, cfg_b])` therefore
   produced two drivers whose first identities shared their private
   key, CKA_ID, DER bytes, and implicit-CA identifier. Any test that
   exercised multi-token scenarios silently tested the same key
   under two labels.
2. Changing an identity's label or subject CN at a given index did
   not rotate the derived key — only the index mattered. That made
   it impossible to distinguish "rename identity" from "reuse key".

Fix: fold the token identity (slot + label + manufacturer + serial)
into the HKDF salt and fold the full identity spec (label,
subject_cn, key_type, can_sign, can_derive, is_ca) into the
per-identity HKDF info, using version-tagged length-prefixed framing
so independent fields cannot collide across different serializations.

Two new helpers do the framing:

    push_len_prefixed(buf, bytes)  // u64-le length + raw bytes
    push_bool(buf, v)              // single 0/1 byte
    push_key_type(buf, key_type)   // tagged enum emission

Two new context builders:

    soft_token_derivation_context(config) -> Vec<u8>
        tag "FCP-SOFT-TOKEN-CONTEXT-V2"
          + config.slot (u64-le)
          + config.token_label
          + config.manufacturer
          + config.serial

    identity_derivation_info(index, spec) -> Vec<u8>
        tag "FCP-SOFT-TOKEN-IDENTITY-V2"
          + (index as u64)
          + spec.label
          + spec.subject_cn
          + spec.key_type
          + spec.can_sign
          + spec.can_derive
          + spec.is_ca

`materialize_identities` now takes `&SoftTokenConfig`, computes the
token context once, and passes it (along with `(index, spec)`) to
`derive_identity`, which constructs the per-identity HKDF info via
`identity_derivation_info` and expands:

    HKDF(salt = DEFAULT_MASTER_SEED, ikm = token_context)
        .expand(info = identity_derivation_info(index, spec), out = 32 bytes)

`implicit_ca_signing_key_bytes` and `implicit_ca_certificate` take
the same `token_context: &[u8]` so the implicit CA per token is
distinct. The implicit CA's CKA_ID is derived from
`blake3(tag "FCP-SOFT-TOKEN-IMPLICIT-CA-ID-V2" || token_context)`
instead of a constant literal, so the CKA_ID differs per token too.

Module-level doc is updated ("All key material is derived from a
fixed seed via HKDF… Each identity gets a unique sub-seed derived
from the token identity, the identity specification, and its
index.") — still deterministic, but now "deterministic given the
whole config" rather than "deterministic given the index alone".

## Regression coverage

- `identity_spec_changes_key_material_at_same_index`: two tokens
  sharing every config field except the identity label at index 0.
  Asserts `signing_key_bytes` AND `id` differ; the label is now a
  real part of the HKDF info, not a nickname.

- `multi_token_harness_assigns_distinct_identity_material_per_token`:
  builds a `SoftTokenHarness` with two configs
  (slot 0 / Token-A / serial A001 vs slot 1 / Token-B / serial B001)
  and asserts:
    - identity[0].id differs between tokens ("different soft tokens
      must not expose colliding CKA_IDs"),
    - identity[0].certificate.der_bytes differs ("different soft
      tokens must not expose identical certificates"),
    - extra_certificates[0].id (the implicit CA) differs ("implicit
      CA objects must be token-specific").

No production caller needs to change; every public API still takes
the same inputs. Callers that hard-coded the old derived material in
fixtures (searchable via the tag `FCP-SOFT-TOKEN-IDENTITY-V2` vs
the prior `fcp-soft-token-identity`) will need to re-snapshot, but
no such fixtures exist in-tree.
